title: Amauropelta saxicola
taxonomy: Amauropelta saxicola is classified within the family Thelypteridaceae, a family of ferns commonly known as the marsh ferns or thelypterid ferns. This species belongs to the genus Amauropelta, which consists of several species of ferns found primarily in the Americas. Amauropelta saxicola is a specific species within this genus, with its classification further detailed within the kingdom Plantae. The classification of Amauropelta saxicola is also reflected in its ranking within the botanical hierarchy as a species, genus, family, order, class, division, and kingdom. intro: Amauropelta saxicola, also known as the saxicole fern, is a type of vascular plant belonging to the fern family (Thelypteridaceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may aid in reducing inflammation and protecting against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Amauropelta saxicola include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Amauropelta saxicola is used to make infusions, teas, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health issues, including fever, respiratory problems, and gastrointestinal disorders.
